title: cistus retrofractus
intro: Cistus retrofractus, also known as rockrose or sunrose, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Cistus retrofractus has been used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. Cistus retrofractus can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, or ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, burns, and minor wounds.
